name: AI Receptionist System
professionalExpectations: An AI receptionist system must separate intake, conversation policy, handoff, escalation, logging, and human review.
Voice, phone, SMS, email, calendar, CRM, and live customer interactions must remain gated until explicitly approved.
The existing AG Digitalz AI Receptionist repo is a separate product project and must not be modified by this archetype record.
The first version must prove conversation scope, fallback behavior, and reviewability before live channels exist.
minimumQualityBar: Conversation boundaries, allowed actions, prohibited actions, escalation triggers, and fallback copy are documented.
No phone, voice, SMS, email, booking, CRM, payment, or production customer-data connection exists without approval.
Transcripts, memory, and contact details are classified before storage or processing.
Prompt-injection and untrusted-user-input risks are handled explicitly.
commonModules: Business intake profile, conversation policy, knowledge boundaries, and escalation rules.
Transcript review, handoff queue, appointment-intent model, and blocked live-channel gates.
Prompt-injection handling, safe refusal rules, and sensitive-data redaction plan.
Future connector plan for phone, SMS, email, calendar, CRM, and booking systems.
uxExpectations: Operator views should make conversation status, risk, escalation, and next action visible.
Configuration should be plain-language and reviewable by the owner.
Testing views should clearly distinguish simulated conversation from live customer interaction.
Logs should be readable, redacted where appropriate, and linked to audit expectations.

components: Local policy and conversation test records
GitHub-governed source for any future implementation
Existing Postgres only after approved transcript and customer-data handling
n8n only after approved modular workflow and backup plan
Phone, SMS, email, or voice providers only after owner approval
recommendedStack.notes: Start with local policy, conversation simulation, and review records. No live phone or voice action is allowed by default.

phases.name: Policy And Scope
goals: Define receptionist role, allowed topics, blocked actions, escalation, and data handling.
Map prompt-injection and live-channel approval gates.

phases.name: Offline Simulation
goals: Run local conversation scenarios with audit-friendly transcripts.
Validate fallback, refusal, and handoff behavior.

phases.name: Approved Live Channel
goals: Add one owner-approved live channel only after rollback, monitoring, and data rules are ready.
Keep human review and emergency stop behavior available.

knownPitfalls: Connecting phone, SMS, email, or booking before safety policy is validated.
Letting the assistant make commitments the business has not approved.
Storing transcripts or customer details before classification, retention, and redaction rules exist.
Mixing this archetype with the existing AI Receptionist product repo without explicit project approval.
approvalGates: Owner approval is required before phone, voice, SMS, email, calendar, booking, CRM, n8n, database, or deployment connection.
Owner approval is required before using production customer data, transcripts, credentials, paid services, or live conversations.
Owner approval is required before modifying the existing AI Receptionist repo.
Owner approval is required before any AI-generated message is sent to a real customer or external party.
qualityChecklist: Conversation policy, escalation, prohibited actions, and fallback behavior are explicit.
Prompt-injection, sensitive-data, transcript, and audit rules are documented.
Offline scenarios validate the first workflow without live channels.
All phone, voice, SMS, email, booking, CRM, and deployment actions remain blocked until approval.
whatToBuildFirst: Offline conversation scenario runner or record model.
Transcript review and handoff state model.
Approval-gate map for one future live channel.
whatNotToOverbuildEarly: Live voice or phone integration before policy and handoff quality are proven.
Multi-channel automation before one channel is safe.
Advanced personalization or memory before data handling rules exist.
Autonomous booking, quote, payment, or commitment actions before explicit approval.
stopConditions: Stop before phone, voice, SMS, email, booking, CRM, n8n, database, deployment, domain, DNS, paid service, or credential connection.
Stop before live customer interaction or production customer-data handling.
Stop before touching the existing AI Receptionist repo without a separate approved scope.
notes: Uses the existing intake archetype ID for AI-tool routing while defining the AI Receptionist product pattern. It does not modify the separate AI Receptionist repo.
